arguments.task: Open official Apify sources and extract evidence for: 1) one-line product description from docs or product pages, 2) all documented API authentication methods from first-party docs only, 3) whether API access is self-serve free/trial/paid/gated using official pricing or docs, 4) API surface type and breadth from official API reference/docs, 5) whether an official MCP server exists, 6) rate limit notes. Prefer canonical pages that load in browser and return page text. Visit docs.apify.com pages for API, authentication, rate limits, and MCP if available, and apify.com/pricing if needed. Return the exact URLs visited with concise evidence snippets.

input.thought: Browse official Apify documentation and pricing pages to gather evidence for structured research fields.

raw_result.description: Platform for developing, running, and sharing web scraping and automation tools in the cloud.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official API docs document API token auth via Authorization: Bearer <token> header (recommended) or token query parameter; they also document agentic payment headers for unauthenticated AI-agent access using PAYMENT-SIGNATURE (x402) or skyfire-pay-id (Skyfire), mapped here as other.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing page shows a self-serve Free plan at $0/month with $5 credit and no credit card required, alongside paid self-serve plans.

api_surface.breadth: Public HTTP API with broad platform coverage including Actors, actor builds/runs/versions, Tasks, Datasets, Key-value stores, Request queues, Webhooks, Schedules, Users/usage, and Proxy-related operations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official MCP server exists at mcp.apify.com for connecting AI agents to Apify Actors and documentation.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official API docs state a global limit of 250,000 requests per minute per user/IP, with typical per-resource limits around 60 requests/second and some endpoints up to 400 requests/second; HTTP 429 is returned on limit exceed.
raw_result.notable_signals: Well-structured official docs, explicit auth documentation, self-serve free plan, broad REST API, and an official MCP endpoint are all strong signals for immediate toolkit buildability.

result.description: Platform for developing, running, and sharing web scraping and automation tools in the cloud.
result.auth_notes: Official API docs document API token auth via Authorization: Bearer <token> header (recommended) or token query parameter; they also document agentic payment headers for unauthenticated AI-agent access using PAYMENT-SIGNATURE (x402) or skyfire-pay-id (Skyfire), mapped here as other.
result.gating_reason: Official pricing page shows a self-serve Free plan at $0/month with $5 credit and no credit card required, alongside paid self-serve plans.

api_surface.breadth: Public HTTP API with broad platform coverage including Actors, actor builds/runs/versions, Tasks, Datasets, Key-value stores, Request queues, Webhooks, Schedules, Users/usage, and Proxy-related operations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official MCP server exists at mcp.apify.com for connecting AI agents to Apify Actors and documentation.

result.rate_limit_notes: Official API docs state a global limit of 250,000 requests per minute per user/IP, with typical per-resource limits around 60 requests/second and some endpoints up to 400 requests/second; HTTP 429 is returned on limit exceed.
result.notable_signals: Well-structured official docs, explicit auth documentation, self-serve free plan, broad REST API, and an official MCP endpoint are all strong signals for immediate toolkit buildability.
